steps.title: Write both as y
steps.content:
Solve each equation for $y$:
$$
8x+y=-11
$$
$$
y=-8x-11
$$
and
$$
2x^2=y+341
$$
$$
y=2x^2-341
$$

steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
At an intersection, the two $y$-values are equal:
$$
2x^2-341=-8x-11
$$
$$
2x^2+8x-330=0
$$
$$
x^2+4x-165=0
$$

steps.title: Find a listed solution
steps.content: Factor the quadratic: $$ x^2+4x-165=(x+15)(x-11) $$ So the possible $x$-values are $-15$ and $11$. The listed possible value is $-15$. The answer is A.
